hypotheses.root_cause: The IMU is addressed through a valid Zephyr node, but the first I2C transaction happens while the LSM6DS3TR-C power path or bus lines are not yet electrically stable.
evidence_for: serial.log shows an nRF TWIM I2C line error before LSM6DSL reports failed reboot and failed chip initialization
zephyr.dts contains a board-specific LSM6DS3TR-C regulator enable GPIO and startup-delay-us property
The upstream XIAO BLE Sense DTS models the ST IMU as an I2C device at reg 0x6a, so the failure is after DTS node selection rather than a missing node
evidence_against: Current sanitized zephyr.dts already contains startup-delay-us = <3000>, so an old-board-DTS timing bug is not proven from this packet alone
No I2C logic trace or regulator enable waveform is available
missing_evidence: scope_trace for IMU enable rail and first I2C transfer
exact Zephyr/NCS version and board revision from the failing device
hypotheses.verification_step: Capture IMU enable GPIO, sensor VDD, SDA, and SCL from reset through the first LSM6DSL register read; then repeat with startup-delay-us increased and with any board-local pull-up override removed.
hypotheses.expected_observation: If this hypothesis is true, the failing capture shows I2C activity before the IMU rail is stable or malformed SDA/SCL levels, and the delay/pull-up fix removes the TWIM line error.
hypotheses.fix_if_confirmed: Keep the board DTS regulator startup delay at the empirically verified minimum, remove incorrect I2C pull-up overrides, and preserve the waveform plus debug packet as a golden regression case.

hypotheses.root_cause: The Zephyr DTS or Kconfig path does not enable the LSM6DSL driver instance that the sample expects.
evidence_for: This is a common Zephyr sensor bring-up failure mode when overlays or board targets are wrong
evidence_against: zephyr.dts contains lsm6ds3tr-c@6a with status okay, compatible st,lsm6dsl, reg 0x6a, and irq-gpios
.config has CONFIG_SENSOR=y, CONFIG_I2C=y, CONFIG_GPIO=y, CONFIG_LSM6DSL=y, and CONFIG_LSM6DSL_TRIGGER_GLOBAL_THREAD=y
missing_evidence: locally generated zephyr.dts from the exact failing checkout
full build log from the failing machine
hypotheses.verification_step: Run dts_probe_check.py on build/zephyr/zephyr.dts and kconfig_check.py on build/zephyr/.config for the LSM6DSL node and required symbols.
hypotheses.expected_observation: If this hypothesis is true, one of the DTS status/compatible/reg/irq-gpios or CONFIG_SENSOR/I2C/GPIO/LSM6DSL checks fails.
hypotheses.fix_if_confirmed: Fix the board target, overlay application, node status, compatible string, I2C address, interrupt GPIO property, or missing Kconfig symbol before modifying driver code.

hypotheses.root_cause: The sample's trigger or logging path consumes more stack than the configured runtime budget on this board.
evidence_for: The sample uses trigger mode and floating-point formatted output
evidence_against: The observed failure occurs during LSM6DSL initialization before sustained trigger/FIFO handling
.config sets main and system workqueue stacks to 2048 bytes
missing_evidence: stack high-water marks from a running board
hypotheses.verification_step: Enable Zephyr thread analyzer or stack sentinel and capture stack usage after the driver initializes successfully.
hypotheses.expected_observation: If stack is the root branch, stack margin collapses or a stack sentinel/assert appears after initialization rather than during the first I2C transaction.
hypotheses.fix_if_confirmed: Increase the specific thread stack that underflows and preserve the stack snapshot in the golden packet.
